findings.title: No timeout on 200-combo sync optimizer; thread pool worker held for minutes
findings.why_it_matters: The endpoint is a synchronous FastAPI def route, so Starlette runs it in its AnyIO thread pool. With 200 combos, the first call fetches OHLCV from yfinance (1 network round-trip, cached via TTL for subsequent combos), then runs 200 sequential compute_indicators + simulation loops. On a slow machine or large date range (5y of daily data with multi-indicator rules), this easily takes 30-60s. During that time one thread pool worker is pinned. There is no asyncio.wait_for guard, no client-abort detection, and no progress streaming. If the browser tab closes mid-run, the server loop continues burning CPU until all 200 combos complete. The builder flagged this in NEXT_RUN.md but left it as a future-pass item.
evidence: Line 59: def optimize_backtest(req: OptimizeRequest) -> OptimizeResponse: — sync def, no timeout parameter
Lines 87-97: for combo in product(...) iterates up to 200 times calling run_backtest(modified) each iteration with no break or timeout mechanism
run_backtest at backtest.py:296 is also a plain def and calls _fetch() (yfinance network I/O on first call per unique key) then runs the full simulation loop
NEXT_RUN.md explicitly flags: C22: No timeout on optimizer endpoint — 200 backtests on a slow machine could take 30-60s. Consider adding asyncio.wait_for or a streaming response in a future pass.
findings.suggested_fix: Short-term: add a wall-clock deadline check inside the loop (if time.monotonic() - start > 60: break) and return partial results with a timed_out: bool field in OptimizeResponse. Medium-term: convert to a streaming SSE response so the client gets progressive results and can cancel.

findings.title: Redundant model_copy(deep=True) per combo before _apply_param
findings.why_it_matters: req.base.model_copy(deep=True) at line 90 allocates a full copy of StrategyRequest (including all rule lists), then passes it to _apply_param which immediately does another model_copy(deep=True) at backtest_sweep.py:53. The caller copy is discarded. At 200 combos this is 200 wasted Pydantic deep-copies per request. For typical StrategyRequest sizes this adds ~10-50ms of unnecessary allocation and GC pressure, scaling with rule-set complexity.
evidence: backtest_optimizer.py line 90: modified = req.base.model_copy(deep=True) — allocates copy
backtest_optimizer.py line 92: modified = _apply_param(modified, path, value) — passes copy to _apply_param
backtest_sweep.py line 53: modified = base.model_copy(deep=True) — _apply_param immediately deep-copies its base argument, making the caller copy dead allocation at the start of every iteration
findings.suggested_fix: Remove line 90 entirely. In the loop, initialize modified = req.base, then for each (path, value) call modified = _apply_param(modified, path, value). Since _apply_param always deep-copies on entry (backtest_sweep.py:53), req.base is never mutated and the extra allocation is eliminated.

residual_risks: The optimizer runs all combos with the same (ticker, start, end, interval) key, so _fetch() returns a cache hit after combo 1. However if a user runs the optimizer near a 2-min intraday TTL boundary, a mid-run cache miss could cause a yfinance re-fetch partway through, adding unpredictable latency to a specific combo without surfacing an error.

invalidateBots() is called after every mutation in BotControlCenter. Rapid successive mutations (start+stop+delete) could produce a burst of overlapping /api/bots refetch requests. React Query deduplicates in-flight fetches so correctness is unaffected, but bots list could show stale data for up to one poll cycle if a mutation completes while a prior refetch is already in-flight.
testing_gaps: No test for optimizer endpoint behavior under high combo count — a test with 200 trivial combos should assert wall-clock completion within a reasonable limit, and would need updating if a deadline mechanism is added.

No test confirming _fetch() cache hit behavior across optimizer combos — should verify _fetch() is called exactly once (not 200 times) for a fixed (ticker, start, end, interval) across all combos in an optimize run.
